atoms.semantic_claim: The old builder context owns a shared provider-backed SPC verifier, initializes it during node builder context construction with an in-memory executor registry of capacity 100, fails node startup if environment-based verifier construction fails, and exposes the verifier to later launch/add-on wiring.

destinations.evidence: Current Ethereum add-ons build an ExecutorRegistry with capacity 100, construct SpcVerifier::new_from_env_simple(...)? with the node provider, and pass the verifier into ritual_async_rpc::module. The ? means verifier construction failure aborts add-on launch instead of allowing the async-result RPC without verifier state.

The async-result RPC loads the canonical AsyncJobTracker job, rebuilds the SPC request from canonical job context, syncs the verifier registry from state at the commit block, calls spc_verifier.verify_single, and only then calls pool.submit_async_result. Missing registry/job data, invalid requests, invalid proofs, and verifier errors all return failed async responses before pool state is advanced.

Block verification still includes the SPC phase: scheduled verification, scheduled censorship, async commitments, settlements, verify_spc_calls, nonce locks, and sequencing rights all run from parent state. This covers verifier-side block acceptance without needing an AddOnsContext-held SpcVerifier object.

atoms.equivalence_argument: Confirmed after mechanical review: the builder-context-owned verifier object is not exact-preserved, but the runtime lifecycle properties that matter for active traffic are preserved at the ethereum/node add-ons boundary. The verifier is constructed with ExecutorRegistry capacity 100, construction is fail-closed via ?, async-result RPC verifies before mutating pool state, and block verification performs state-backed SPC checks. The old BuilderContext accessor is an API/ownership difference, not a missing FSM transition.
atoms.negative_test_candidate: Keep startup/RPC regressions proving verifier construction failure aborts add-on launch and invalid SPC submissions do not call pool.submit_async_result.
atoms.notes: Reviewed 2026-05-11: builder-owned verifier API is absent by design; active verifier startup and RPC/block-verification FSM are covered elsewhere.

summary: Reviewed and downgraded: the old BuilderContext verifier object is not exact-preserved, but verifier startup, registry capacity, fail-closed behavior, RPC verification, and block-verification phases are covered by current ethereum/node and ritual-block-verification code.
notes: Destination BuilderContext remains verifier-free and const-shaped.
The verifier is instead constructed in EthereumAddOns::launch_add_ons with ExecutorRegistry::new(100) and SpcVerifier::new_from_env_simple(...)? .
The ? preserves fail-closed add-on launch semantics for the active async-result RPC surface.
No unresolved verifier-lifecycle proof remains; the difference is ownership/API shape.
